substep.title: Select Methodology
substep.action: Load references/research/investigation-methodologies.md. Match the question shape to one methodology (domain / market / technical / codebase / general) per the selection table. When the question spans shapes, pick the dominant one and fold secondary concerns in as sub-questions.

substep.title: Instantiate Sub-Questions
substep.action:
The methodology supplies sub-question GENERATORS — templates, not finished questions. Instantiate each generator against the concrete topic. "Industry structure: key players" for a topic of European EV charging becomes "Who are the key players in the European EV charging market and how concentrated is it?"
Each instantiated sub-question carries: its text, its scope, and the child subagent its shape routes to (per references/research/child-subagent-dispatch.md). Seed sub-questions with what the mentioned sources from step 1c already established — do not re-ask what is already known.

substep.title: Scope-Too-Large Gate
substep.action:
A single comprehensive investigation produces ONE coherent document. If the instantiated decomposition would need more than one methodology to answer well, or yields so many sub-questions and waves that no single document would cohere (rough guide: beyond ~8-10 sub-questions or ~3 waves), the question is a program of research, not one investigation.
In that case, emit to stdout and stop:
SCOPE_TOO_LARGE Reason: Suggested sub-investigations: - - - <...>
The caller (or user) dispatches these as separate investigations.

check.template-output:
Present the plan for confirmation before any dispatch: - Selected methodology and why - The instantiated sub-questions, grouped into waves - Which sub-questions route to web research (flag for the approval gate in step 3)
[a] Adjust the plan [c] Continue to dispatch [y] YOLO
